title: Clinical Fitness
body: In the evolutionary analysis of clinical trials, does the probability of reaching result-posting status differ significantly between African and European research ecosystems? This metadata audit evaluated one thousand interventional trials across four global regions using the status-module and results-section metadata from the ClinicalTrials.gov API v2 database. Researchers performed a survival-to-results analysis and reported the fitness score as the primary comparative estimand for research sustainability and successful data discharge. The primary result showed that African trials exhibit a surprisingly high fitness score of forty-one percent, significantly exceeding the European average of eight percent during the audit period. This high survival rate in Africa likely reflects a hardy research model where limited starting resources necessitate a high probability of completion for initiated studies. These findings suggest that African research ecosystems are highly efficient at converting initiation into results despite low innovation volume. Interpretation is limited by the exclusion of trials registered on non-public databases today. - DOI: 10.156/clinical-fitness - Date: 2026-03-28 - Type: research - Mode: evolutionary
ai_transparency: LLM assistance was used for drafting and language editing. The author reviewed and edited the manuscript and takes responsibility for the final content.
